name: Sendero del Valcorba (PRC®-VA 21) — circular trailrunning Santibáñez de Valcorba
region_intro: Jogging routes around Santibáñez De Valcorba traverse the Valcorba stream valley, characterized by dense vegetation and the gentle slopes of its surrounding uplands. The area features established paths that wind through wooded páramos, offering varied terrain for runners. The Valcorba stream itself is a central natural element, contributing to the lush environment found along many running routes. These running trails provide opportunities to explore the natural beauty of the region. Best jogging routes around Santibáñez De Valcorba The most popular jogging route is Sendero de la Atalaya (PRC-VA 49) — circular trailrunning desde Traspinedo, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail that takes 38 minutes to complete. This route features a moderate elevation gain of 417 feet (127 meters) through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Sendero del Valcorba (PRC-VA 21) — circular trailrunning Santibáñez de Valcorba, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.3 km) path. This trail follows the Valcorba stream, offering views of the valley and surrounding uplands. Local runners also love the Montemayor de Pililla loop from Montemayor de Pililla, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Jogging around Santibáñez De Valcorba is defined by the Valcorba stream valley, dense vegetation, and wooded uplands. The network offers options for different ability levels, with all routes currently rated as moderate. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 8 runners have used komoot to explore Santibáñez De Valcorba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Santibáñez de Valcorba?
A: Santibáñez de Valcorba offers a selection of 5 dedicated running routes. These trails are designed to help you explore the beautiful Valcorba stream valley and its surrounding uplands.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in this region?
A: The jogging trails in Santibáñez de Valcorba primarily traverse the Valcorba stream valley, featuring dense vegetation and the gentle slopes of its surrounding &lt;em&gt;páramos&lt;/em&gt; (uplands). You'll find paths winding through wooded areas, offering varied terrain that is generally moderate in difficulty.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Santibáñez de Valcorba?
A: Yes, several of the running routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120158955/sendero-de-la-atalaya-prc-va-49-circular-trailrunning-desde-traspinedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de la Atalaya (PRC-VA 49)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120176373/sendero-del-valcorba-prc-va-21-circular-trailrunning-santibanez-de-valcorba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Valcorba (PRC®-VA 21)&lt;/a&gt; are both circular trail running options.

Q: What are some interesting sights or landmarks I might encounter on a run?
A: While running, you can enjoy the natural beauty of the Valcorba stream and the wooded uplands. For specific landmarks, consider routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4011321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Hermitage of Traspinedo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge of Olivares de Duero&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features &lt;em&gt;La Fuente Vieja&lt;/em&gt; (Old Fountain) next to a municipal park, and the &lt;em&gt;Paseo de Las Bodegas&lt;/em&gt; with its unique underground wineries, which add to the regional character.

Q: Are the running trails in Santibáñez de Valcorba suitable for families?
A: The running routes in Santibáñez de Valcorba are generally rated as moderate, making them accessible for active families. The natural setting along the Valcorba stream and through wooded areas provides a pleasant environment for outdoor activity. Always consider the distance and elevation gain for younger or less experienced family members.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging routes?
A: Many natural trails in Spain are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. While specific rules for Santibáñez de Valcorba's trails aren't detailed, it's generally advisable to respect local wildlife and other trail users. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on these running routes?
A: The running routes around Santibáñez de Valcorba feature moderate elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120158955/sendero-de-la-atalaya-prc-va-49-circular-trailrunning-desde-traspinedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de la Atalaya&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of about 417 feet (127 meters), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120176373/sendero-del-valcorba-prc-va-21-circular-trailrunning-santibanez-de-valcorba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Valcorba&lt;/a&gt; involves approximately 469 feet (143 meters) of ascent. These provide a good workout without being overly strenuous.

Q: Are there any longer distance running options available?
A: While all current routes are moderate in length, you can combine sections or explore the longer options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montemayor de Pililla loop&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longer routes at 9.8 kilometers (6.1 miles), offering a more extended running experience through the local landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Santibáñez de Valcorba?
A: Santibáñez de Valcorba, with its lush environment, is enjoyable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings or evenings can also be ideal to avoid the midday heat, allowing you to fully appreciate the 'luxuriance of the wooded uplands'.

Q: Are there any specific projects or initiatives related to nature trails in the area?
A: Yes, Santibáñez de Valcorba is involved in the 'Miconaturval' project. This initiative aims to establish and promote a route focused on mycology and nature within the Valcorba valley, highlighting the region's commitment to maintaining and enhancing its natural trails for activities like running and hiking.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Santibáñez de Valcorba?
A: More than 10 runners have used komoot to explore Santibáñez de Valcorba's varied terrain. They often praise the natural beauty of the Valcorba stream valley and the well-defined paths that wind through the wooded uplands, making for an enjoyable and scenic running experience.
tips: The parish church of Santibáñez, with an initial medieval layout, is the most representative and important building in the town. Its original construction dates back to the 13th century, and some remains from that time are still preserved, indicating a clearly Mudejar style, also known as brick Romanesque.
Point within the PRC-VA 20 Sendero de la Sombría starting in SANTIBÁÑEZ DE VALCORBA. About 7 km. circular and of low difficulty.

waypoints.name: Church of San Juan de Santibáñez
